element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn efficient harvesting; forests used as defensive walls in early game when combat forces are small
Resource storage and worker unit production; foundation for all subsequent structures
Each farm supports up to four units; players learn supply cap constraint prevents unlimited army expansion
Produces non-magical combat units; introduces army composition and unit types (melee, ranged, mounted, siege)
Expands tactical options beyond melee; requires understanding of unit positioning and range
Advanced barracks units requiring research; introduce specialized roles (cavalry, siege warfare)
Two types of magic buildings per faction; Clerics heal and protect, Necrolytes raise skeleton troops and grant invulnerability
Energy blasts, wide-range destruction spells, summoning abilities (water elemental vs. demonic units); require prerequisite building assistance
Players must optimize existing platoon composition; emphasizes tactical efficiency over economic growth
Can be used as troops or present obstacles; complicates map control and resource gathering
Includes conquering rebels, rescuing besieged towns, assassinating faction leaders (e.g., killing Orc chief's daughter)

typical_relief_triggers: Transitioning from defense to army production
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy army approaching before defenses established
Limited food supply preventing army expansion
Surprise attacks on exposed resource gatherers
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical build-defend-attack rhythm; tension escalates from economic vulnerability to military engagement; limited-forces missions create sharp tension spikes; multiplayer introduces sustained competitive pressure

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Random map generator for multiplayer and skirmish modes allows exploration of procedurally varied terrain

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to resource collection, basic infrastructure, and worker management; minimal military pressure; forests act as natural barriers
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of combat units, ranged warfare, and basic spellcasters; players learn unit composition and tactical positioning; first significant enemy engagements
difficulty_curve.late_game: Advanced spellcasters, unit upgrades, and complex mission objectives; limited-forces scenarios test tactical efficiency; multi-pronged attacks or assassination targets increase complexity
notable_spikes: First meaningful enemy army encounter requiring coordinated defense and offense
Introduction of advanced spellcasters with summon abilities
Assassination missions combining stealth, positioning, and resource constraints

notable_level_patterns.description: Early missions establishing resource gathering infrastructure while defending against initial enemy incursions; forces players to balance economic growth with military presence
Core Warcraft paradigm: gather resources → build infrastructure → produce army → assault enemy base; varies from straightforward destruction to complex objectives
No unit production allowed; players must optimize existing platoon through positioning, upgrades, and tactical engagement; emphasizes skillful small-force management
Non-standard win conditions: rescuing besieged towns, extracting units from enemy camps, defeating faction leaders, conquering rebel forces; increases narrative variety and tactical demands
Wild creatures on map create dynamic obstacles or opportunities; can be eliminated, avoided, or recruited as troop substitutes; increases environmental complexity
